name: Multi-Channel Notification Preferences & Delivery Log
description: Per-user notification preferences (which channels/events to receive) and a delivery history log, backing a real Slack/Teams webhook dispatcher wired to platform events like churn-risk alerts. FIXED 2026-07-13: real frontend UI shipped, plus 3 collateral backend defects found and fixed while wiring it (see notes).

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-13: Settings page gained a real 'Team Alert Channels (Slack / Teams / Webhook)' card (frontend/user-dashboard.html:1937-1971) with Slack/Teams/custom-webhook URL inputs, 4 event-subscription checkboxes (scan.critical/case.created/case.escalated/health.churn), Save/Test buttons, and a live delivery-log table. Backing JS (:5208-5294): loadNotifChannels()/saveNotifChannels()/testNotifChannels()/loadNotifLog(), wired into showPage('settings') so it loads automatically. Explicitly labeled to disambiguate from the pre-existing, unrelated 'Alert Notifications' card (email/Telegram CVE-risk alerts) directly above it. Before this fix: repo-wide search across frontend/*.html for 'notifications/preferences' returned zero matches.

navigation.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-13: reachable via the existing Settings nav item (frontend/user-dashboard.html:974), which was already in the primary sidebar — no new nav entry needed since this is a Settings sub-section, not its own page.

test_coverage.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-13: workers/test/notificationPlatformDashboard.test.mjs (11 tests, real D1 via node:sqlite) — proves the auth gate genuinely rejects anonymous callers on all 4 endpoints (was dead code, see notes), proves real per-user isolation (two users' webhooks don't collide, DB-row-count asserted), proves the eventType='*' wildcard broadcast actually bypasses subscription filtering (was a no-op comparison, see notes), and proves 'health.churn' delivers to a default-subscribed customer while the old mismatched 'CHURN_RISK_ALERT' string would not (regression guard). Full worker suite (274 files/2853 tests) green. Additionally browser-verified live via Playwright against the real frontend/user-dashboard.html served locally with contract-accurate mocked API responses (response shapes copied directly from workers/src/handlers/threatGraph.js and workers/src/handlers/notificationPlatform.js source, not guessed): preferences load into the form, event checkboxes reflect real subscriptions, delivery log renders real rows, Send Test Notification round-trips and displays the result, zero console/JS errors. This browser pass is what caught the showPage() wiring defect documented in notes — a unit-test-only pass would have missed it.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/notificationPlatform.js and workers/src/auth/middleware.js read and edited directly; workers/test/notificationPlatformDashboard.test.mjs (11/11 passing) and workers/test/authAliases.test.mjs (2 new tests) read/run directly; frontend/user-dashboard.html edited directly, inline <script> re-parsed with node --check, and click-through verified via Playwright/Chromium against contract-accurate mocked responses (11/11 checks passing, zero console errors). Full worker suite: 274 files/2853 tests green. Registry validator: 0 failures/0 warnings. SEO structure lock: 31/31 pages. customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-deploy production smoke test with a real account (not yet performed against live D1) and because rbac/subscription_gating remain intentionally unenforced (pre-existing design choice, not touched by this fix). | Direct re-verification: real 'Team Alert Channels' card confirmed in frontend/user-dashboard.html Settings page, wired to loadNotifChannels()/saveNotifChannels()/testNotifChannels()/loadNotifLog(), discoverable via the existing Settings nav item. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-14 (Enterprise Capability Completion Program triage): independently re-confirmed via source (not the registry label) that this frontend/navigation work is real, live, and reachable. Priority corrected from P2 (backend exists, frontend missing) since that is no longer true.

notes: The backend delivery mechanism (Slack/Teams webhooks firing on real events) already worked and was undersold, not broken — the original gap was purely that no customer could configure it. Wiring the frontend surfaced 3 real, previously-undiscovered defects, all fixed in the same pass: (1) authCtx.id was never populated anywhere in the auth layer (only user_id/userId existed), so every real customer's webhook config and delivery history silently collided onto a shared 'unknown' D1 row, and the '!req.user' auth gate was permanently dead code since the router always sets a truthy req.user (real principal or anonymous IP-fallback) — fixed with a `.id` alias in workers/src/auth/middleware.js's withAuthAliases() (same fix shape as that function's pre-existing `.role` fix) plus switching the gate to the real isRealUser() helper; this transparently also fixes 4 sibling handlers with the identical bug (reportingEngine.js, workflowAutomation.js, globalSearch.js, productAnalytics.js), not re-certified here since they're outside this capability. (2) deliverNotification()'s subscription check compared eventType against itself when eventType==='*', making the documented wildcard-broadcast intent (developerOnboardingHandler.js:231's explicit comment 'wildcard — ensures delivery regardless of subscription prefs for new users') a no-op — every new-user welcome email, password-recovery email, and this capability's own Test Notification button silently delivered to nobody; fixed with an `eventType !== '*'` short-circuit. (3) revenueIntelligenceHandler.js's churn-risk alert emitted eventType:'CHURN_RISK_ALERT', which never matched the canonical 'health.churn' subscription key used everywhere else (DEFAULT_PREFS, all 3 schema SQL files, this capability's own Settings checkbox, workflowAutomation.js's template) — real churn-risk alerts had silently delivered to zero customers since inception; fixed by aligning the one outlier to the established convention. Separately, a real UI-layer bug was found and fixed by actually browser-testing rather than trusting the unit tests alone: the Global Intel Graph and Intelligence Preview nav items' auto-load calls (intelGraphSummary()/intelPreviewFeatured()) lived in the nav item's onclick instead of inside showPage(), so a deep link (?tab=intel-graph) would show a permanent loading spinner — moved into showPage() itself, matching the more robust pattern this Settings card already used.

name: In-App Notification Bell
description: A homepage bell icon showing a badge count and dropdown of recent alerts.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-09. CDB_NOTIF's _fetch() (frontend/index.html, in the CDB_NOTIF IIFE) was rewired from GET /api/v1/alerts + GET /api/realtime/stats (global threat intel, plus a _synthesizeAlerts() fallback that fabricated fake 'personal' alerts from platform-wide CVE counts — both removed) to GET /api/notifications/log, the customer's own notification_log-backed history CAP-NOTIF-001's backend maintains. Maps the real response shape ({log:[{id,channel,event_type,subject,status,sent_at,created_at}],total}) to the existing render item (subject -> title, channel -> description). Gated on the same JWT presence check (_authToken(), reading the pre-existing cdb_v13_jwt key) the module already used for its Authorization header, so a signed-out visitor gets an honest '🔒 Sign in to see your notifications' empty state instead of either a crash or someone else's-looking data — matches the real backend, which requires a resolved session (workers/src/index.js:3241-3244 sets request.user via resolveAuthV5) and otherwise degrades gracefully rather than 401ing. Verified live via Playwright: signed-out state shows the sign-in message; signed-in state (mocked GET /api/notifications/log) renders the real subject line and no longer shows any global-threat-intel content.

navigation.evidence: The bell icon is in frontend/index.html's primary header, always visible.

test_coverage.evidence: No committed automated test covers the CDB_NOTIF frontend object (inline page JS, not an importable module). Verified this pass via an ad-hoc Playwright click-through (not committed to the repo) against contract-accurate mocked responses; not a permanent regression lock — a real gap, flagged as a follow-up (e.g. a committed Playwright/browser test suite) rather than fixed in this pass.

verification.evidence: Source fix read directly against workers/src/handlers/notificationPlatform.js:176-190's real handleNotificationLog response shape. Playwright click-through of the real frontend/index.html confirmed: (1) signed-out visitors see an honest sign-in prompt with zero network call to a protected endpoint; (2) a signed-in session (simulated JWT, mocked GET /api/notifications/log response shaped exactly like the real handler's SQL SELECT columns) renders the real subject as the item title and no longer shows the old fabricated CVE-count alerts. Full backend suite (188 files/2012 tests) green post-fix — this was a frontend-only change. NOT YET verified against the live deployed backend/D1 notification_log table — customer_journey_complete stays false pending a post-merge production smoke test with a real account that has notification_log rows.

notes: Distinct from frontend/user-dashboard.html's own separate, unrelated client-only alert array, which docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:222 also flags as disconnected from notification_log — not addressed in this pass, remains a related open item. Two adjacent accessibility defects surfaced and fixed in the same pass while touching this widget: (1) the severity-badge palette's LOW tier fell through to MEDIUM's cyan background tint while keeping distinct gray text (3.44:1 contrast, failing WCAG AA) — given its own tint plus the page's existing --text-muted color (7.08:1); (2) the shared p4Toast() helper appended plain divs with no ARIA live region, silent to screen readers — now role="status" aria-live="polite". Both verified via axe-core (zero WCAG2A/AA violations in the notification panel post-fix).

name: Outbound Webhooks (Developer + Organization)
description: Let a customer register a webhook URL to receive real-time event notifications. Previously two separate, competing implementations; the developerPortal implementation was retired 2026-07-15 (PR #254) as unused/incomplete dead code, leaving the enterpriseAutomation one as the sole system.

frontend.evidence: workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:856-904 implements full CRUD plus a manual test-fire against a developer_webhooks table (9-event catalog per docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:223), but that catalog is not wired to any automatic dispatcher — webhooks only ever fire when a user manually clicks test. Separately, workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js:22-243 implements a second, independent schema (org_webhooks plus a webhook_delivery_log audit table) that DOES fire automatically on matching events (:231-243), but is a completely separate table/code path from the developer_webhooks system — a customer using one system has no visibility into or relationship with the other. No frontend page was found calling either system's CRUD endpoints in this pass. Verified 2026-07-08 by reading both files' SQL directly.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry calls either webhook system's routes because no page does.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports the webhook-specific functions from either workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js or workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js directly in a way targeting this feature specifically.

verification.evidence: PR #254 (commit 0ebf7a7, merged 2026-07-15) retired workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js's webhook subsystem in full (zero frontend callers, no dispatch function, unsent signing) after re-verification, leaving workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js's /api/auto/webhooks* as the sole system. Confirmed via git log/git show 0ebf7a7 and a fresh read of the current developerPortal source (webhook routes/handlers no longer present).

notes: Documentation-drift correction (2026-07-15): this entry previously described the pre-PR#254 two-system duplication as an open architectural problem; that duplication is resolved. Real residual gap, not drift: the enterpriseAutomation dispatchWebhookEvent function has zero production callers repo-wide (only workers/test/webhookVocabularyAndDeliveryLogs.test.mjs calls it) — the surviving system still only fires on a manual "Test" click, not on real platform events. That, plus the still-missing frontend, is why operational_status stays NOT READY.

name: Transactional & Lifecycle Email Engine
description: Branded transactional email (signup, password reset, payment confirmation/failure, coupon redemption, suspicious login) plus multi-step drip sequences, with provider fallback and a dead-letter queue for failed sends.

frontend.evidence: This capability's customer surface is the inbox, not a page — evidence is that real triggers call it from real customer actions: workers/src/handlers/payments.js:1104 calls sendPaymentFailedEmail on a failed webhook; per docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:127-134, the engine implements 9 drip sequences, 20+ branded templates, a Resend-to-MailChannels provider fallback cascade, cron-driven retry, and a dead-letter queue (email_dlq table) with bounded retry, shipped 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: N/A (delivered via email, not a navigable page); triggered automatically by real account/payment events.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/emailDlqAndTemplates.test.mjs and workers/test/emailWiringTask3Phase1.test.mjs import from workers/src/services/emailEngine.js — confirmed by import-statement grep, 2026-07-08. UPDATED 2026-07-15: workers/test/enterpriseLeadDripStep4.test.mjs (new, 2 tests, real node:sqlite D1) covers runDripAutomation's step-to-template dispatch for the 'enterprise' sequenceId specifically — previously zero test file exercised DRIP_SEQUENCES/getSequenceTemplate/runDripAutomation's actual send behavior at all (the one prior reference, workers/test/proposalBola.test.mjs, mocks runDripAutomation away entirely).

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/payments.js:1104 read directly to confirm a real trigger exists (not just a defined-but-uncalled function, the exact class of mistake this registry exists to prevent); workers/test/emailDlqAndTemplates.test.mjs and workers/test/emailWiringTask3Phase1.test.mjs import statements confirmed. UPDATED 2026-07-15 (Customer Lifecycle Completion Program, Phase 6): found and fixed a real gap by tracing DRIP_SEQUENCES against getSequenceTemplate/runDripAutomation's actual dispatch switch in workers/src/services/emailEngine.js rather than trusting the 'GA' status. The 'enterprise' sequence (real trigger: workers/src/handlers/growth.js:86, enrolled on every scan-detected enterprise-domain lead) declares steps:[0,1,3,5,7] (5 touches) and DELAY_MAP.enterprise was already sized to 5 entries, but the legacy dispatch switch shared with 'welcome'/'trial_expiry' only implemented 4 templates (templateDay0-3) — confirmed via templateDay3's own subject copy, which explicitly reads 'This is the last email in your free trial sequence'. Step index 4 fell through to the no-op default branch, silently marking the row 'completed' via advanceSequence's nextStep>=4 threshold without ever calling sendEmail(). Fixed by adding templateEnterpriseLeadDay7 (new copy, distinct from the similarly-named but differently-triggered templateEnterpriseDay7 used by the separate enterprise_nurture/inquiry sequence) and wiring it to step index 4, gated to seqId==='enterprise' only.

notes: Multi-channel expansions (SMS, real WhatsApp Business API send, push) remain tracked as their own product-backlog gaps, not defects in this capability. UPDATED 2026-07-15: the enterprise-sequence 5th-touch gap above is now fixed — see test_coverage and verification.evidence. advanceSequence()'s completion check (workers/src/services/emailEngine.js, `isDone = nextStep >= 4`) is hardcoded rather than derived from each sequence's real step count; for sequences with fewer than 4 real steps (upgrade_nudge, subscription_activated, assessment_delivered, mssp_onboarded) this causes 1-2 harmless wasted cron passes before the row is marked completed — no wrong or duplicate email is ever sent, so this was left as a documented code-quality note rather than fixed in this pass (touching it is a shared-code change affecting every sequence, a larger blast radius than the single-sequence fix above).

name: Subscription Renewal Reminder Automation
description: ADDED 2026-07-15 (Customer Lifecycle Completion Program, Phase 6): daily cron-driven pipeline that seeds a renewal_queue from active subscriptions renewing within 35 days, then sends T-30/T-14/T-7/T-1-day reminder emails plus an expired-subscription reactivation email via Resend. Previously existed in the repo (v35.1 Phase 5 P0) but had zero registry entry and, per this pass's verification, a real defect that silently defeated its own T-30/T-14 claim — now fixed as part of this entry's creation.

frontend.evidence: This capability's customer surface is the inbox, not a page. Real trigger: workers/src/index.js:9193-9209 calls buildRenewalQueue() (7-day window) then seedRenewalQueue35d() + runRenewalAutomation() unconditionally inside the daily cron block (event.cron === '0 23 * * *'), confirmed by direct read — not just defined-but-uncalled functions.

navigation.evidence: N/A (delivered via email, not a navigable page); triggered automatically by real subscription lifecycle state (subscriptions.current_period_end).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/renewalQueueSeeding.test.mjs (new, 4 tests, real node:sqlite-backed D1) covers seedRenewalQueue35d directly. runRenewalAutomation's interval-matching and expired-recovery logic remain untested — flagged as the next actionable gap for this capability, not covered by this pass.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/renewalEngine.js read directly in full. Found and fixed a real defect: seedRenewalQueue35d's query filtered on `s.expires_at`, a column that has never existed on the `subscriptions` table (workers/schema_master.sql:3131-3160 defines `current_period_end`, not `expires_at`; confirmed via full-text grep across every workers/*.sql file, no ALTER TABLE ever added it). The resulting D1 'no such column' error was silently swallowed by `.all().catch(() => ({ results: [] }))`, so this function has always returned `{ queued: 0 }` in production — meaning only the T-7/T-1 reminders (populated by workers/src/services/v24/billingEngine.js's separate 7-day-window buildRenewalQueue) could ever actually fire; T-30 and T-14 were dead code despite the module's own header comment claiming all four intervals work. Fixed to use `s.current_period_end`, added the missing `s.cancel_at_period_end = 0` filter (matching buildRenewalQueue's existing guard, so an already-cancelled customer is never sent a false 'your subscription will auto-renew' email), and corrected `user_id` to be selected/bound from `s.user_id` instead of silently falling back to the customer's email address in that column.

notes: Documentation Drift: the module's own header comment (workers/src/handlers/renewalEngine.js:3, 'Phase 5 P0: Renewal reminders at T-30, T-14, T-7, T-1 days') asserted all four intervals worked; this pass found the T-30/T-14 half was never reachable in production due to the defect above, with zero test coverage to catch it — the exact pattern docs/audit-history/ warns about, caught here before a full CLCP-audit doc repeated the same unverified claim. runRenewalAutomation's own logic (interval matching, expired-recovery, Resend send) was read and traced manually but has no direct test coverage yet; recommended as the next increment for this capability rather than folded into this fix (single production problem per PR).

name: Paying-Customer Churn Prevention / Win-Back Engine
description: ADDED 2026-07-15 (Customer Lifecycle Completion Program, Phase 6): cron-driven pipeline that identifies paying customers inactive 7+ days (no scan in the window) and queues a discount win-back email (20% for 2 months, or 30% for 3 months if silent 30+ days) via the same KV-queue → processUpsellEmailQueue → dispatchEmail → Resend path used by other automationEngine.js jobs. Resolves the mission brief's open question on the separate `enterprise_winback` sequence (docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:285-286, 'verify it's wired to real churn events') by finding and fixing the actual paying-customer win-back path; `enterprise_winback` itself remains scoped to pre-purchase proposal rejections (workers/src/handlers/proposalGenerator.js), a separate and already-working capability, not touched here. Previously existed in the repo with zero registry entry and a defect that silently defeated it completely (0 at-risk customers found, ever) — now fixed as part of this entry's creation.

frontend.evidence: This capability's customer surface is the inbox, not a page. Real trigger confirmed by direct read: workers/src/index.js's 'v8.2 Revenue Automation Pipeline' cron block (inside ctx.waitUntil, unconditional — not gated on event.cron) imports and calls runAutomationCron(env, event.cron) on every cron tick; runAutomationCron (workers/src/services/automationEngine.js:39-61) calls processUpsellEmailQueue(env) and runChurnPrevention(env) directly and unconditionally, not just defined-but-uncalled functions. dispatchEmail's buildEmailFromTemplate('retention', ...) (workers/src/services/automationEngine.js:601-616) produces a real discount-offer email and is the same function already proven live for other queue types.

navigation.evidence: N/A (delivered via email, not a navigable page); triggered automatically by real customer activity state (scan_history recency joined against leads.funnel_stage).

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/churnPreventionQuery.test.mjs (new, 5 tests, real node:sqlite-backed D1) covers runChurnPrevention directly: flags an inactive paying customer and queues a real retention-email KV entry with correct payload; does not flag a free-plan lead; does not flag a lead that never converted (funnel_stage still 'visitor'); does not flag a customer active within 7 days; does not re-queue a customer with an existing pending retention email. processUpsellEmailQueue's KV-drain → dispatchEmail path and runAutomationCron's orchestration were read and traced manually but have no direct test coverage yet — flagged as the next actionable gap for this capability, not covered by this pass (single production problem per PR).

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/automationEngine.js read directly in full. Found and fixed a real defect: runChurnPrevention's query filtered on `l.status = 'active'`, a column that has never existed on the `leads` table (workers/schema_master.sql:1730-1760 defines `funnel_stage` and `stage`, not `status`; confirmed via full-text read of the CREATE TABLE statement, no ALTER TABLE ever added it). The resulting D1 'no such column' error was silently swallowed by `.all().catch(() => ({ results: [] }))`, so `at_risk` has always been 0 in production — zero win-back emails have ever been queued for any inactive paying customer since this code existed, on any plan (Starter/Pro/Enterprise/MSSP). Fixed to use `l.funnel_stage = 'customer'`, the established 'currently paying, converted' signal used identically elsewhere (workers/src/services/lifecycleEngine.js:136 sets it on conversion; workers/src/handlers/revenueKPI.js:53 reads it as the MRR 'converted' count; workers/src/handlers/leads.js:211 and workers/src/index.js:9247 both exclude 'customer' and 'churned' together to mean 'still a pre-conversion lead', confirming the two values are mutually exclusive on this column and no additional churned-exclusion filter is needed.

notes: Same bug shape as CAP-NOTIF-005's renewal-queue defect (wrong/nonexistent column name, silently swallowed by a permissive .catch(), zero test coverage to catch it) — found by applying that same pattern-match during this session's broader Phase 6 customer-success audit, not by re-litigating CAP-NOTIF-005 itself. A related, still-open gap surfaced by the same audit and deliberately NOT fixed here (different production problem): `evaluateUpsellTriggers()` (workers/src/services/upsellEngine.js:149-223) correctly computes in-app usage-limit warnings (90% scan quota, 80% API quota) and is exposed at a real route (POST /api/growth/upsell/evaluate), but has zero callers anywhere — no cron, no frontend. Wiring it up requires a product decision on where in the request lifecycle it should fire (on scan completion? on dashboard load?), which is UI/UX scope requiring human sign-off, not a backend-only fix.
